title: Stock market crash scenario: Oil prices above $100, AI-spending rebellion
excerpt: There was nowhere to hide for investors on Thursday as tech earnings and the Iran war dragged stocks lower.
content_html:
For the past few months, the stock market has been dominated by twin narratives: (1) the long-term viability of the AI trade and (2) the impact of the Iran war.

On a day in, day out basis, the narrative has rotated. A multi-billion-dollar AI-infrastructure collaboration might juice tech stocks one day. The next day that same group might rally because of data showing inflation is cooling.

They've also served as cover for one another. If a major chipmaker issues a disappointing sales forecast, that might be offset by a deescalation in US-Iran tensions.

Long story short, there's usually something to either excite investors, or at least temper any negativity. And that's helped keep major indexes up within shouting distance of record highs — albeit with some volatility along the way.

On Thursday, that volatility reared its angry head in a way that felt different, because the sell-off was a byproduct of both headwinds flaring up at the same time.

The trading session ended with the S&P 500 down 1.2% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 losing nearly 2%. The damage was even deeper in AI-adjacent areas like the Magnificent 7, which collectively tanked 4%.

Ultimately, the episode amounted to two of the market's most important catalysts combining to form a giant arrow lower for stocks. In many ways, the situation offers a glimpse at a worst-case scenario of sorts for stocks. Let's unpack both parts:

1. The Iran war sends oil surging again

What's happened: After a blissful and brief period back below pre-war levels, crude oil prices have surged back above $100 as the US and Iran conflict has reignited. This, in turn, has sent inflation expectations higher. Investors have been on high alert for this after seeing the impact of a prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure reflected in CPI data.

A hotter inflation forecast has investors starting to accelerate expectations for rate hikes, which have historically been kryptonite for stocks. This entire chain of events helps explain the market's bad day on Thursday.

What's next: As always, the ball is in the court of the Trump administration, and their negotiation counterparts from Iran. The market has shown itself to be desperate for a peace deal, and more than willing to price one in. All they need are some signs of progress, which have been scant to non-existent lately.

2. AI-trade investors decide they're sick of all the spending

What's happened: On Thursday, Alphabet and Tesla were the latest tech companies to find out that impatient traders are getting tired of capex spending and are instead looking for tangible results. Chipmakers including TSMC found this out earlier this earnings season in what now looks like a warning of what was to come.

Alphabet tumbled 7% after raising its spending forecast, cancelling out strong earnings and immense growth in its AI-focused Google Cloud business. Tesla, meanwhile, fell 15% after pledging more spending and missing profit estimates.

What's next: The rest of the main Magnificent 7 hyperscalers — Meta, Microsoft, and Amazon — report earnings next week. It's safe to say at this point that, no matter what numbers the company reports, none of it will matter if they boost capex spending. And since companies have been unabashed in their deployment, we could be looking at another series of AI-stock sell-offs.

blocks.html_en: • Tesla

content_html_zh:
在过去的几个月里，股市一直被双重叙述所主导：（1）人工智能行业的长期生存能力和（2）伊朗战争的影响。

日复一日，叙事在不断轮换。 一项价值数十亿美元的人工智能基础设施合作有一天可能会提振科技股。 第二天，由于数据显示通胀正在降温，同一群体可能会上涨。

他们还互相充当掩护。 如果一家主要芯片制造商发布令人失望的销售预测，那么美伊紧张局势的缓和可能会抵消这一影响。

长话短说，通常会有一些东西可以让投资者兴奋，或者至少缓和任何负面情绪。 这有助于主要股指保持在历史高点的范围内——尽管一路上出现了一些波动。

周四，这种波动以一种不同的方式出现，因为抛售是两种逆风同时爆发的副产品。

该交易日结束时，标准普尔 500 指数下跌 1.2%，以科技股为主的纳斯达克 100 指数下跌近 2%。 在与 AI 相邻的区域（例如 Magnificent 7），损坏程度甚至更深，总共损失了 4%。

最终，这一事件导致市场两个最重要的催化剂结合起来，形成了股市的巨大下跌箭头。 从很多方面来说，这种情况让我们看到了股市最坏的情况。 让我们拆开这两个部分：

1. 伊朗战争令油价再度飙升

发生了什么：在短暂回到战前水平以下之后，随着美国和伊朗冲突重新爆发，原油价格已飙升至 100 美元以上。 这反过来又推高了通胀预期。 在看到消费者物价指数数据反映出霍尔木兹海峡长期关闭的影响后，投资者对此保持高度警惕。

通胀预测升温让投资者开始加速加息预期，而这在历史上一直是股市的克星。 这一系列事件有助于解释周四市场表现不佳的原因。

接下来：一如既往，球掌握在特朗普政府和来自伊朗的谈判对手手中。 市场已经表明自己迫切希望达成一项和平协议，并且非常愿意为此定价。 他们所需要的只是一些进展的迹象，而最近这些迹象几乎不存在。

2. 人工智能贸易投资者认为他们厌倦了所有的支出

发生了什么：周四，Alphabet 和特斯拉是最新一家发现不耐烦的交易者厌倦了资本支出，转而寻求切实成果的科技公司。 包括台积电在内的芯片制造商在本财报季早些时候发现了这一点，现在看起来像是对即将发生的事情的警告。

Alphabet 在提高支出预期后股价下跌 7%，抵消了其以人工智能为重点的谷歌云业务的强劲盈利和巨大增长。 与此同时，特斯拉在承诺增加支出且未达到利润预期后股价下跌 15%。

接下来是什么：Magnificent 7 的其他主要超大规模提供商——Meta、微软和亚马逊——将于下周公布收益。 目前可以肯定地说，无论公司报告的数字是多少，如果他们增加资本支出，那么一切都无关紧要。 由于各公司毫不掩饰其部署，我们可能会看到另一系列人工智能股票的抛售。

乔是《商业内幕》的执行编辑，也是每日市场通讯《First Trade》的作者。 在此注册。他负责新闻编辑室的市场和投资报道，此前还负责管理经济和金融团队。 他于 2017 年 4 月开始在 Business Insider 担任记者。在加入 BI 之前，他是彭博社的股票记者，还在专注于外汇、债券和并购的团队工作过。 在加入 Bloomberg 之前，他曾在 CIBC World Markets 和 Navigant Capital Advisors 担任投资银行分析师。Joe 拥有斯坦福大学新闻学硕士学位和圣路易斯华盛顿大学工商管理学学士学位。
